summary: Security review accepts the M5 Opportunity Detail status-write slice. The PATCH /api/opportunities/{id} path accepts only saved, discarded, and build_next; rejects new, built, malformed JSON, missing ids, and missing opportunities with sanitized responses; writes only Opportunity.status through a structured Prisma update; and does not add raw SQL, broad mutation, delete/data-loss behavior, env/secret/provider/package/schema changes, auth/session expansion, or an MVP Spec route.

security_checklist.notes: lib/opportunities.ts:42 defines the mutation allow-list as saved, discarded, and build_next only. lib/opportunities.ts:593 validates updates against that list, while tests/opportunities.test.ts:263 asserts saved/discarded/build_next pass and built/new fail.

app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:50 returns generic INVALID_JSON; app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:83 returns generic VALIDATION_ERROR; app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:96 returns generic NOT_FOUND without echoing ids or exception text. tests/opportunities.test.ts:343, tests/opportunities.test.ts:369, and tests/opportunities.test.ts:394 cover invalid status, malformed JSON, and not-found behavior.

app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:122 extracts only body.status before calling the update boundary. lib/opportunities.ts:380 uses db.opportunity.update with data: { status } at lib/opportunities.ts:382 and a narrow select at lib/opportunities.ts:383. tests/opportunities.test.ts:313 proves extra request fields do not reach the update boundary.

security_checklist.item: No raw SQL or broad mutation
security_checklist.notes: Focused scan found no queryRaw, executeRaw, updateMany, createMany, or deleteMany hits in the reviewed implementation files. The only Opportunity write is the structured Prisma status update at lib/opportunities.ts:380.
The implementation adds no Opportunity delete path. Discard is represented as a status value only, and README.md:339 documents that the slice has no irreversible deletes.

security_checklist.item: No secret, env, provider, package, or schema changes
security_checklist.notes: Focused scan found no process.env, lib/env import, provider setup, OpenAICompatibleClient, SERP_PROVIDER, SESSION_SECRET, or related secret/provider wiring in the reviewed implementation files. Protected-path status check showed no changes under package.json, package-lock.json, prisma, lib/env.ts, lib/db.ts, lib/schemas.ts, app/api/scans, app/api/radar-tasks, services, or agents.

security_checklist.item: No auth/session expansion beyond local policy
security_checklist.notes: Focused scan found no cookies, headers, NextAuth, auth, or session implementation hits in the reviewed implementation files. README.md:339 records the local single-admin assumption and explicitly says this slice does not add sessions, auth, public multi-user workflow, audit history, or discard reasons.

security_checklist.item: No MVP Spec route or generation
security_checklist.notes: No app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec route directory exists. Focused scan found no mvp-spec or MvpSpec hits in the reviewed implementation files, and README.md:350 documents MVP Spec generation, copy behavior, and the mvp-spec route as non-goals.

The endpoint remains intentionally unauthenticated for the local MVP, matching the accepted project policy documented in README.md:339 and README.md:354. No public deployment, CORS, multi-user ownership, or user-isolation model was introduced.

tests_run.result: Reviewed working tree: modified README.md, lib/opportunities.ts, tests/opportunities.test.ts; untracked app/api/opportunities, app/opportunities/[id], components/OpportunityDetail.tsx, components/OpportunityStatusControls.tsx, and implementation handoff. Product changes are within the task's allowed paths.
PASS: 14 focused opportunity tests passed across 3 suites, including mutable status allow-list, extra-field no-op boundary, invalid/new/built rejection, malformed JSON, and safe not-found errors.
PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully. Existing nonfatal local Windows @next/swc optional-binary warnings were printed before fallback build continued.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings for README.md, lib/opportunities.ts, and tests/opportunities.test.ts.

tests_run.command: rg prohibited security patterns over reviewed implementation files
tests_run.result: PASS: no prohibited raw SQL, broad update/delete, env/secret/provider, auth/session, or MVP-spec hits in reviewed implementation files.
PASS: no app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec route directory exists.

tests_run.command: git status --short -- package.json package-lock.json prisma lib/env.ts lib/db.ts lib/schemas.ts app/api/scans app/api/radar-tasks services agents
tests_run.result: PASS: no protected package, schema, env/db/schema helper, scan API, radar-task API, service, or agent path changes were present.
PASS: security reviewer handoff JSON validates.

decisions: ACCEPT: The status write path is a narrow local mutation boundary over Opportunity.status only.
Accepted the distinction between read-side status support and write-side status support: new and built may remain readable/filterable, but cannot be written through this PATCH path.
Accepted generic 404 handling for missing or invalid ids because the response does not leak internal ids or Prisma details and matches the existing local API response style.
Accepted the client-side fetch in OpportunityStatusControls because it only calls the same local PATCH route with one allowed status value selected from fixed UI actions.
Accepted the unauthenticated route only within the documented local single-admin MVP policy; production exposure still requires a later auth/session and deployment policy slice.
No product code was modified during this review. Only the requested security review handoff was added.
risks: The PATCH endpoint is intentionally unauthenticated for the local single-admin MVP. Public deployment remains out of scope and requires a later auth/session gate.
The status update has no audit trail, discard reason, ownership model, or multi-user isolation by design for this slice.
Opportunity ids are returned in successful status responses, which is acceptable for local admin operation but should be revisited if this endpoint becomes public.
Next build continues to print existing nonfatal local Windows SWC optional-binary warnings.
requested_action: Proceed with QA review for M5-OPPORTUNITY-DETAIL-STATUS-001. Security review accepts the implementation with local-MVP residual risks noted and no requested changes.
